metrics.top_n: Number of visible bid/ask book levels used to build each client's weighted depth profile for DWI/MSCI computation.
metrics.kappa: Depth-kernel execution-risk protection parameter; larger values increase the protection-from-execution component for levels away from the best quote.
metrics.lambda: Depth-kernel visibility-decay parameter; larger values down-weight levels farther from the best quote more aggressively.
metrics.epsilon: Small numerical stabilizer used in denominators to avoid division by zero without changing ordinary finite ratios.
metrics.window_seconds: Post-execution clock-time window used to measure liquidity collapse, matched cancellations, and price-response diagnostics after a passive fill.
metrics.max_deceptive_order_age_seconds: Maximum allowed age of a pre-existing same-client opposite-side candidate order before the execution; older orders are excluded as stale background liquidity.
metrics.gamma_grid: MSCI thresholds used to aggregate event-level MSCI exceedances into client-level MCPS repetition scores.
metrics.tick_size: Explicit minimum price increment for depth-distance calculations; null means infer it from the reconstructed best bid/ask quote panel.
metrics.state_client_mode: Controls which clients get DWI state rows: 'all' keeps all clients, while 'passive-fill-clients' keeps only clients that can enter passive-fill surveillance events to reduce memory.
metrics.compact_state: When true, omits per-level diagnostic columns from client_metric_time_series while keeping the aggregate DWI/L_bid/L_ask state needed by downstream outputs.
metrics.empirical_depth_kernel: Optional empirical depth-kernel parquet/csv artifact. When set, rank weights replace the scalar kappa/lambda kernel in DWI/MSCI weighting.

grid.depth_grid: Set of top-N depths to evaluate in the multidepth sensitivity grid.
grid.kappa: Depth-kernel execution-risk protection parameter used for every depth-grid run unless overridden on the CLI.
grid.lambda: Depth-kernel visibility-decay parameter used for every depth-grid run unless overridden on the CLI.
grid.epsilon: Small denominator stabilizer used for every depth-grid run.
grid.window_seconds: Post-execution clock-time window used for every depth-grid run.
grid.max_deceptive_order_age_seconds: Maximum age of candidate deceptive orders before the execution in every depth-grid run.
grid.gamma_grid: MSCI thresholds used to compute MCPS for each depth in the grid.
grid.tick_size: Explicit tick size for grid runs; null means infer it from the quote panel.
grid.make_dashboard: When true, writes a spoofing_metric_dashboard.html for each depth run in addition to parquet and summary outputs.
grid.empirical_depth_kernel: Optional empirical depth-kernel parquet/csv artifact applied to every depth-grid run when present.

event_review.top_n: Number of visible book levels to reconstruct and display in event-review queue snapshots.
event_review.pre_window_seconds: Clock-time interval before the selected execution shown in the event-review log and LOB queue context.
event_review.post_window_seconds: Clock-time interval after the selected execution shown in the event-review log and LOB queue context.
event_review.queue_snapshot_mode: Queue output density: 'all' writes snapshots for every event in the review window, while 'key-events' writes only nearest pre/execution/post snapshots to reduce dashboard size.

production_readiness.msci_threshold: Event-level MSCI cutoff used when counting client-session MSCI exceedances for alert features.
production_readiness.min_events: Minimum number of repeated matched-withdrawal events required before emitting a human-review alert.
production_readiness.min_mcps: Optional minimum matched-event share required for an alert; 0 disables the share floor so active clients with many matched withdrawals are not dropped only because their denominator is large.
